profile.name: Rajat Srivastav
profile.bio: Fullstack Developer with a love for open source. Always looking for interesting projects to contribute to. Let's connect and build something great!

prs.title: fix(charts): support advanced MCPGatewayExtension configuration

labels.name: review-effort/medium
labels.description: Medium review effort (3): few files, moderate logic

prs.body:
## Summary

The current Helm chart for the `mcp-gateway` hardcodes only a limited subset of the `MCPGatewayExtension` specification. This restricts users from configuring important production settings such as `httpRouteManagement`, `sessionStore`, and `trustedHeadersKey` directly through Helm values.

This change updates the extension template to support conditional rendering for all supported `v1alpha1` spec fields while keeping the generated YAML clean when values are not provided.

Closes: #928

## Changes

### Template Updates

Updated `charts/mcp-gateway/templates/mcpgatewayextension.yaml` to conditionally render the following fields using `with` and `if` blocks:

* `httpRouteManagement`
* `sessionStore.secretName`
* `trustedHeadersKey.generate`
* `trustedHeadersKey.secretName`

### Values File Updates

Updated `charts/mcp-gateway/values.yaml` to include the new configuration keys with descriptive comments for easier discovery and customization.

## Verification Steps

Create a temporary file named `test-values.yaml`:

```yaml
mcpGatewayExtension:
  httpRouteManagement: Disabled

  sessionStore:
    secretName: "my-custom-session-secret"

  trustedHeadersKey:
    generate: Enabled
```

Run:

```bash
helm template my-test charts/mcp-gateway -f test-values.yaml
```

Expected output in the rendered `MCPGatewayExtension` resource:

```yaml
spec:
  httpRouteManagement: "Disabled"

  sessionStore:
    secretName: "my-custom-session-secret"

  trustedHeadersKey:
    generate: "Enabled"
```

### Negative Test

Run `helm template` without the additional values and verify that:

* No empty fields are rendered
* No `null` values appear
* Optional blocks are omitted entirely when unset


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Introduced new advanced configuration options for the MCP Gateway: control HTTP route management behavior, specify session store credential secrets, and manage trusted headers signing keys through auto-generation or custom secret references.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Clean and validate RHoMIS processed dataset
prs.body: Cleaned the RHoMIS dataset as per the cleaning contract, added reproducible ETL and validation scripts, and generated processed outputs ready for EDA.

prs.title: Add initial RHoMIS data extraction notebook
prs.body:
Added the initial 01_extraction.ipynb notebook for the RHoMIS Analytics project.

Changes included:
- Added basic dataset loading setup
- Added raw data path configuration
- Added initial dataset inspection steps
- Added basic data quality checks without cleaning or transformation
- Kept the notebook limited to extraction only

No cleaning, EDA, or statistical analysis has been performed in this notebook.

prs.title: added glare hover animation
prs.body:
##  Description
This PR adds a sophisticated CSS animation called "Glare Hover" featuring a 3D rotating card with an animated glare/shine effect that moves across its surface, simulating holographic light reflection.

##  Animation Details

**Name**: Glare Hover  
**Folder**: `rajatrsrivastav-glarehover`  
**GitHub Handle**: @rajatrsrivastav

### Visual Description
A premium-style card that:
- Rotates in 3D space (both X and Y axes)
- Features a moving glare/shine effect across its surface
- Simulates realistic light reflection
- Floats up and down gently
- Displays a beautiful purple-to-pink gradient
- Creates a holographic/collectible card aesthetic
- Maintains perspective depth throughout

### Shine Effect
- Diagonal light sweep (45-degree angle)
- White gradient with varying opacity
- 4-second continuous movement
- Creates realistic reflection illusion
- Traverses from bottom-left to top-right

### Techniques Used
- CSS `perspective` for 3D effect
- `transform: rotateY() rotateX()` for 3D rotation
- `translateY()` for floating animation
- Linear gradient for shine effect
- 8-second rotation cycle
- Layered animations (card rotation + shine movement)
- Deep box shadows for depth

## Checklist

- [x] Uses **only HTML and CSS** (no JavaScript)
- [x] No images or SVG files
- [x] Animation is **looping** continuously
- [x] No user interaction required
- [x] Folder contains exactly **3 files**: `index.html`, `styles.css`, `meta.json`
- [x] Proper folder naming: `rajatrsrivastav-glarehover`
- [x] `meta.json` correctly formatted
- [x] No modifications outside my contribution folder
- [x] Original code created by me

## 📁 Files Added

```
Art/rajatrsrivastav-glarehover/
├── index.html
├── styles.css
└── meta.json
```

## meta.json Content

```json
{
  "artName": "Glare Hover",
  "githubHandle": "rajatrsrivastav"
}
```

## Key Features

- 8-second 3D rotation cycle
- 4-second shine movement cycle
- Realistic light reflection
- Card dimensions: 320px × 450px
- Premium gradient background
- Floating animation
- Professional depth shadows
- Holographic aesthetic

## Visual Preview

The animation creates a premium, collectible card effect with a realistic glare that sweeps across the surface while the card gently rotates in 3D space, perfect for showcasing products or creating engaging UI elements.

---

prs.title: added  Pixel Transition Animation
prs.body:
## Description
This PR adds a dynamic CSS animation called "Pixel Transition" featuring a 4x4 grid of pixels that transform in a sequential wave pattern with color and shape transitions.

## Animation Details

**Name**: Pixel Transition  
**Folder**: `rajatrsrivastav-pixeltransition`  
**GitHub Handle**: @rajatrsrivastav

### Visual Description
A 4x4 grid (16 pixels total) where each pixel:
- Rotates through 360 degrees
- Scales between 0.7x and 1.3x
- Morphs through different shapes (rounded square → circle → sharp square → circle)
- Transitions through 4 different gradient colors
- Animates with 0.2s staggered delays creating a wave effect
- Creates a mesmerizing pixel art display

### Color Sequence
1. Purple/Blue gradient
2. Pink/Red gradient
3. Cyan/Blue gradient
4. Green/Cyan gradient

### Techniques Used
- CSS Grid layout (4×4)
- Individual `animation-delay` for each pixel (16 unique delays)
- `nth-child()` selectors for precise timing
- Multiple gradient transitions
- Combined transform properties (rotate, scale)
- Border-radius morphing
- 3.2-second animation cycle

## Checklist

- [x] Uses **only HTML and CSS** (no JavaScript)
- [x] No images or SVG files
- [x] Animation is **looping** continuously
- [x] No user interaction required
- [x] Folder contains exactly **3 files**: `index.html`, `styles.css`, `meta.json`
- [x] Proper folder naming: `rajatrsrivastav-pixeltransition`
- [x] `meta.json` correctly formatted
- [x] No modifications outside my contribution folder
- [x] Original code created by me

## Files Added

```
Art/rajatrsrivastav-pixeltransition/
├── index.html
├── styles.css
└── meta.json
```

## meta.json Content

```json
{
  "artName": "Pixel Transition",
  "githubHandle": "rajatrsrivastav"
}
```

## Key Features

- 16 individually timed pixels
- Wave-like sequential animation
- 4 color gradient transitions
- Shape morphing animation
- 3.2-second cycle per pixel
- Professional grid spacing
- Dark gradient background

##  Visual Preview

The animation creates a captivating wave effect across a grid of pixels, each transforming in sequence through colors and shapes, reminiscent of retro pixel art with modern gradient aesthetics.

---

prs.title: added Electric-Border-Animation
prs.body:
## Description
This PR adds a futuristic CSS animation called "Electric Border" featuring a neon-style rotating border with color-shifting effects around a dark box.

##  Animation Details

**Name**: Electric Border  
**Folder**: `rajatrsrivastav-electricborder`  
**GitHub Handle**: @rajatrsrivastav

### Visual Description
A dark rounded box with an electric/neon border effect that:
- Rotates continuously at 360 degrees every 4 seconds
- Features a conic gradient creating the "electric" appearance
- Shifts through the entire color spectrum via `hue-rotate` filter
- Uses layered pseudo-elements for depth
- Creates a glowing, futuristic aesthetic
- Displays cyan/electric blue base color

### Techniques Used
- Conic gradient for circular color sweep
- CSS `filter: hue-rotate()` for color shifting
- `::before` and `::after` pseudo-elements for layering
- Continuous rotation animation
- Black background for maximum contrast
- `overflow: hidden` for clean edges
- Multiple background layers

prs.title: added loop-logo animation
prs.body:
##  Description
This PR adds a futuristic CSS animation called "Logo Loop" featuring a spinning logo with three pulsing concentric rings and a glowing center core, creating a sci-fi/tech aesthetic.

## Animation Details

**Name**: Logo Loop  
**Folder**: `rajatrsrivastav-logoloop`  
**GitHub Handle**: @rajatrsrivastav

### Visual Description
An animated logo system consisting of:
- **Outer Ring**: 280px cyan borders rotating with the logo
- **Middle Ring**: 200px magenta borders counter-pulsing
- **Inner Ring**: 120px green borders synced with outer
- **Center Core**: 50px glowing orb with pulsing shadows
- All rings pulse in and out while the entire logo rotates
- Creates a futuristic tech/sci-fi brand aesthetic

### Ring Colors
- Outer: Cyan (#00f3ff)
- Middle: Magenta (#ff00ff)
- Inner: Green (#00ff88)
- Core: White-to-cyan gradient with glow

### Techniques Used
- Continuous 360-degree rotation (10 seconds)
- Three separate pulsing animations at different timings
- `box-shadow` for neon glow effects
- Multiple layered shadows for depth
- Border-only circles (transparent backgrounds)
- Radial gradient for core
- Reverse animation direction for middle ring
- Staggered animation delays (0s, 0.5s, 1s)

## Checklist

- [x] Uses **only HTML and CSS** (no JavaScript)
- [x] No images or SVG files
- [x] Animation is **looping** continuously
- [x] No user interaction required
- [x] Folder contains exactly **3 files**: `index.html`, `styles.css`, `meta.json`

```
└── meta.json
```

## meta.json Content

```json
{
  "artName": "Logo Loop",
  "githubHandle": "rajatrsrivastav"
}
```

## Key Features

- 10-second rotation cycle
- 3-second ring pulse cycles
- 2-second core pulse cycle
- Multi-layered glow effects
- Three colored rings
- Radial gradient background
- Professional neon aesthetic
- Scale transformations (1.0x to 1.15x)

## Visual Preview

The animation creates a mesmerizing futuristic logo perfect for tech brands, gaming interfaces, or sci-fi projects. The rotating rings with pulsing effects and glowing core create a sense of energy and movement.

## Perfect For

- Tech company logos
- Loading screens
- Brand animations
- Sci-fi UI elements
- Gaming interfaces

prs.title: fix(config): Add missing payment_link config to docker_compose.toml for local SDK loading #9324
prs.body:
## Type of Change
- [x] Bugfix  
- [ ] New feature  
- [ ] Enhancement  
- [ ] Refactoring  
- [ ] Dependency updates  
- [ ] Documentation  
- [ ] CI/CD  

---

## Description 
This PR fixes the issue described in #9324, where the missing payment_link configuration in docker_compose.toml was causing the Hyperloader SDK to fail when running Hyperswitch locally. Without the payment_link block, payment links generated locally were attempting to load the SDK from https://beta.hyperswitch.io instead of the local backend, resulting in SDK load failures and errors in API calls.

### Summary of Changes:
 - Added the missing [payment_link] block in docker_compose.toml. - Set the correct sdk_url to http://localhost:9050/HyperLoader.js for local SDK loading. - Ensured the payment_methods API call points to the local backend rather than the external URL.

### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract  
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema  
- [x] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables  

## Motivation and Context  
As part of my journey to contribute meaningfully to open source, I chose this issue to get hands-on experience with real-world production-grade systems. I’ve been exploring payment infrastructure projects and found Hyperswitch both technically rich and welcoming to contributors.

**Problem:**  
Without the `[payment_link]` config in `docker_compose.toml`, the SDK attempted to load from `https://beta.hyperswitch.io`, causing CORS issues and preventing the payment method list from being retrieved.

**Fixes:**  
[#9324](https://github.com/juspay/hyperswitch/issues/9324) – *Missing payment_link config in docker_compose.toml breaks SDK loading*

## How did you test it?  

✅ **Steps followed for testing:**

- Ran docker compose up to bring up the local services.
-Generated a payment link via the local API using the following curl command:
```bash
curl --location 'http://localhost:8081/payments' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --header 'api-key: snd_5jYonmRK338pDuk1FqxSYfpPNutQUuVFEVV23qzpqzbQ4DYfbksIgdwIkgjb3vnP' \
  --data '{
      "amount": 100,
      "currency": "USD",
      "payment_link": true,
      "profile_id": "pro_ARVaflLlXRmGS5i8jfq5",
      "return_url": "http://www.google.com"
  }'
```
- Opened the generated payment link in a browser and confirmed that:
  - SDK is loaded successfully from `localhost`  
  - The SDK loaded correctly from http://localhost:9050/HyperLoader.js.
  - The payment_methods API call correctly pointed to the local backend.
  - No SDK load failures or network errors occurred.
-  Attached video demo for reference.

[Click here to view the demo video](https://drive.google.com/file/d/13M_js8hlLLDRkWNeAI_VLQ5_1cl-Nzaw/view?usp=sharing)

**Files Changed:**  
-config/docker_compose.toml: Added the missing [payment_link] section with the correct sdk_url for local SDK loading.

Closes #9324
## Checklist

- [x] I formatted the code using `cargo +nightly fmt --all`  
- [x] I addressed lints using `cargo clippy`  
- [x] I reviewed the submitted code  
- [ ] I added unit tests for my changes where possible  
- [x] I have read the `CONTRIBUTING.md`  
- [x] I adhere to the Code of Conduct  
- [x] I have linked the relevant issue  
- [x] I have not included any sensitive information

prs.title: Fix: Add missing payment_method_collect config in docker_compose.toml (#9324)
prs.body: ## Type of Change - [x] Bugfix - [ ] New feature - [ ] Enhancement - [ ] Refactoring - [ ] Dependency updates - [ ] Documentation - [ ] CI/CD --- ## Description This PR fixes a bug where local payment links failed to load the HyperLoader SDK correctly due to a missing configuration in `docker_compose.toml`. ### Changes Made: - Configured `sdk_url` to point to the local instance: `http://localhost:9090/0.16.7/v0/HyperLoader.js` - Enabled payment methods (e.g. `card`, `wallet`, `bank_transfer`) to allow the payment method dropdown to function correctly --- ### Additional Changes - [] This PR modifies the API contract - [ ] This PR modifies the database schema - [x] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables **File Modified:** - `config/docker_compose.toml` ⚠️ Note: `crates/diesel_models/src/schema.rs` was changed automatically during the local setup. No manual edits were made to it. --- ## Motivation and Context As part of my journey to contribute meaningfully to open source, I chose this issue to get hands-on experience with real-world production-grade systems. I’ve been exploring payment infrastructure projects and found Hyperswitch both technically rich and welcoming to contributors. **Problem:** Without the `[payment_link]` config in `docker_compose.toml`, the SDK attempted to load from `https://beta.hyperswitch.io`, causing CORS issues and preventing the payment method list from being retrieved. **Fixes:** [#9324](https://github.com/juspay/hyperswitch/issues/9324) – *Missing payment_link config in docker_compose.toml breaks SDK loading* --- ## How did you test it? ✅ **Steps followed for testing:** - Ran `docker compose up` - Generated a payment link using the `/payments` API - Opened the link in the browser - Verified that: - SDK is loaded successfully from `localhost` - `payment_methods` call is made to `localhost:8080` - Payment methods array is empty (expected), but no error is shown - UI is responsive and does not crash - Shared a working **photo** in below --- ## Checklist - [x] I formatted the code using `cargo +nightly fmt --all` - [x] I addressed lints using `cargo clippy` - [x] I reviewed the submitted code - [ ] I added unit tests for my changes where possible - [x] I have read the `CONTRIBUTING.md` - [x] I adhere to the Code of Conduct - [x] I have linked the relevant issue - [x] I have not included any sensitive information
